description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular agricultural building with a prominent red hip roof made of corrugated metal or tiles, providing ample coverage and weather protection. The walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. On one side, there are large, double barn-style doors with visible black metal hinges, allowing for easy access for livestock and equipment. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground on concrete footings, which helps prevent moisture ingress and provides stability. The open section on one side suggests a sheltered area for feeding or storage, while the rest of the building is enclosed. There are no visible windows, and the eaves are slightly extended, possibly aiding in passive ventilation.
description: A rustic wooden cowshed with a red hip roof, large barn doors, and an open sheltered side. The structure is sturdy, elevated on concrete footings, and lacks windows.
description_view_0: This view shows the main side with large double barn doors and an open sheltered section supported by wooden posts.
description_view_1: This end view highlights the solid wooden wall and the full expanse of the red hip roof, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_2: This side view displays the long wooden wall, the hip roof, and the absence of doors or windows on this side.
description_view_3: This end view shows a plain wooden wall beneath the hip roof, with no openings or features.
attribute_door_style: double sliding barn door, wooden with black metal hinges
